name: Sanctuary Automaton
oracle_text: Vigilance\nWhen ~ enters, salvage 4. (Look at the top four cards of your library. You may put an artifact card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.) If you put an artifact card into your hand this way, scry 2.
flavor_text: Guardian-class constructs were designed to protect the Archive. After 40,000 years, at least something still works.

validation_errors: Oracle text contains what looks like reminder text: "(Look at the top four cards of your library. You may put an artifact card from among them into your hand. Put the rest on the bottom of your library in any order.)"
Combined oracle + flavor text is 376 characters, exceeding the 350-char limit for this card type

design_notes: WU signpost uncommon for the Ancient Technology archetype. A 2/2 vigilant body is efficient at 3 mana and plays well on an early turn. Salvage 4 provides consistent artifact tutoring with a reasonable library depth for uncommon (not overpowered). The conditional bonus (scry 2 if you hit an artifact) rewards deck-building: the more artifacts you play, the more value you get. This incentivizes the archetype without being random or overloaded. Vigilance fits both colors (white gets it, blue's protonium constructs wear it as a design flavor). Flavor supports the "ancient machines still functioning" theme. Power level is well-balanced: you're not guaranteed card advantage on the trigger, and a 2/2 vigilant body for 3 isn't overstatted. Synergizes naturally with other artifact payoffs already in the set (Anomalous Resonance, Cult Relic-Bearer, etc.) and signals the archetype clearly to draft players.
